created_at: 2026-03-30T09:00:00+08:00
author: metaclaw_agent
status: done
---

# New Team Member Onboarding Guide

Welcome to the ProjectX team! This guide will help you get up and running quickly.

---

## Day 1: Account Setup & Access

### 1.1 Required Accounts

Before you start, ensure you have access to:

| Service | Purpose | Request Access From |
|---------|---------|---------------------|
| GitHub (Organization) | Code repository | Alex |
| Slack | Team communication | Auto-invite sent |
| Notion | Documentation | Auto-invite sent |
| Jira | Task tracking | Alex |
| Figma | Design files | Sam |
| AWS Console | Cloud resources | Casey |
| Docker Hub | Container registry | Casey |

### 1.2 Development Machine Setup

#### macOS Setup

```bash
# Install Homebrew (if not already installed)
/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"

# Install required tools
brew install git node@18 postgresql@14 redis docker

# Install VS Code (recommended IDE)
brew install --cask visual-studio-code
```

#### Windows Setup (WSL2 Recommended)

```bash
# Install WSL2 with Ubuntu
wsl --install -d Ubuntu

# Inside WSL2, run:
sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y
sudo apt install -y git nodejs npm postgresql redis-server docker.io
```

#### Linux Setup (Ubuntu/Debian)

```bash
# Update system
sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y

# Install dependencies
sudo apt install -y git curl build-essential

# Install Node.js 18
curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_18.x | sudo -E bash -
sudo apt install -y nodejs

# Install PostgreSQL 14
sudo sh -c 'echo "deb http://apt.postgresql.org/pub/repos/apt $(lsb_release -cs)-pgdg main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/pgdg.list'
wget --quiet -O - https://www.postgresql.org/media/keys/ACCC4CF8.asc | sudo apt-key add -
sudo apt update
sudo apt install -y postgresql-14

# Install Redis
sudo apt install -y redis-server

# Install Docker
curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com -o get-docker.sh
sudo sh get-docker.sh
sudo usermod -aG docker $USER
```

---

## Day 2: Repository Setup

### 2.1 Clone the Repository

```bash
# Create workspace directory
mkdir -p ~/workspace/projectx
cd ~/workspace/projectx

# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/company/projectx.git
cd projectx

# Verify you have access
git remote -v
```

### 2.2 Install Dependencies

```bash
# Install Node.js dependencies
npm install

# Verify installation
npm run verify
```

### 2.3 Environment Configuration

```bash
# Copy environment template
cp .env.example .env

# Edit .env with your local settings
# Ask Casey for development credentials
nano .env

# Required local environment variables:
# - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://localhost:5432/projectx_dev
# - REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379
# - JWT_SECRET=(generate with: openssl rand -base64 32)
# - API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000
```

### 2.4 Database Setup

```bash
# Start PostgreSQL and Redis services
brew services start postgresql@14  # macOS
brew services start redis          # macOS

# On Linux:
sudo service postgresql start
sudo service redis-server start

# Create database
npm run db:create

# Run migrations
npm run db:migrate

# Seed with development data
npm run db:seed

# Verify database connection
npm run db:verify
```

### 2.5 Start Development Server

```bash
# Start the application
npm run dev

# The application will be available at:
# - Web App: http://localhost:3000
# - API Docs: http://localhost:3000/api/docs
```

---

## Key Code Conventions

### 3.1 Naming Conventions

| Type | Convention | Example |
|------|------------|---------|
| **Files** | kebab-case | `user-service.js`, `auth-controller.js` |
| **Components** | PascalCase | `UserProfile.jsx`, `PaymentForm.jsx` |
| **Variables** | camelCase | `userName`, `isAuthenticated` |
| **Constants** | UPPER_SNAKE_CASE | `MAX_RETRY_COUNT`, `API_BASE_URL` |
| **Functions** | camelCase (verb prefix) | `getUserById()`, `handleSubmit()` |
| **Classes** | PascalCase | `UserService`, `PaymentProcessor` |
| **Database Tables** | snake_case, plural | `users`, `payment_transactions` |
| **Environment Variables** | UPPER_SNAKE_CASE | `DATABASE_URL`, `JWT_SECRET` |

### 3.2 Documentation Conventions

- **Markdown files**: Use kebab-case with date prefix for dated documents
  - Format: `YYYYMMDD_description.md`
  - Example: `20260330_sprint_review.md`

- **YAML Frontmatter**: Required for all documentation files
  ```yaml
  ---
  created_at: 2026-03-30T09:00:00+08:00
  author: your_name
  status: in_progress
  ---
  ```

- **Status values**: `pending`, `in_progress`, `done`

### 3.3 Code Style

We use ESLint and Prettier for code formatting:

```bash
# Check code style
npm run lint

# Auto-fix style issues
npm run lint:fix

# Format with Prettier
npm run format
```

**Key Style Rules**:
- 2 spaces for indentation
- Single quotes for strings
- Semicolons required
- Max line length: 100 characters
- Trailing commas in objects/arrays

### 3.4 Git Workflow

```bash
# Create a feature branch
git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name

# Make your changes and commit
# Follow conventional commit format:
# feat: add new feature
# fix: fix a bug
# docs: update documentation
# refactor: code refactoring
# test: add tests
git commit -m "feat: add user authentication"

# Push branch
git push -u origin feature/your-feature-name

# Create Pull Request via GitHub
# Tag Alex as reviewer
```

**Branch Naming**:
- Features: `feature/description`
- Bug fixes: `fix/description`
- Documentation: `docs/description`
- Hotfixes: `hotfix/description`

---

## Commonly Used Commands

### 4.1 Development Commands

```bash
# Start development server
npm run dev

# Start with hot reload (backend only)
npm run dev:api

# Start frontend only
npm run dev:client

# Build for production
npm run build

# Start production build locally
npm run start
```

### 4.2 Database Commands

```bash
# Run migrations
npm run db:migrate

# Rollback last migration
npm run db:migrate:rollback

# Reset database (caution: deletes all data)
npm run db:reset

# Seed database with test data
npm run db:seed

# Open database console
npm run db:console

# Generate new migration
npm run db:migrate:make migration_name
```

### 4.3 Testing Commands

```bash
# Run all tests
npm test

# Run unit tests only
npm run test:unit

# Run integration tests
npm run test:integration

# Run E2E tests (requires dev server running)
npm run test:e2e

# Run tests with coverage
npm run test:coverage

# Run tests in watch mode
npm run test:watch
```

### 4.4 Docker Commands

```bash
# Start all services with Docker Compose
docker-compose up -d

# View logs
d
